This stage is the transport-facing boundary of the system: it sits wherever the client enters or leaves the external Codex service, defining the exact request, response, event, and error shapes that higher-level logic relies on during normal execution. Rather than performing business logic itself, it gives the rest of the codebase a stable, typed contract for HTTP endpoints and realtime streams.

The crate root in codex-api/src/lib.rs is the public entry point, wiring internal modules together and re-exporting the supported client, transport, schema, request, and response types. codex-api/src/common.rs supplies the shared payload models used across multiple endpoints, including Responses API requests, websocket requests, text controls, memory summarization, compaction, and streamed events, so different transports speak the same language. codex-api/src/error.rs standardizes failures into ApiError, letting callers handle transport, protocol, retryable, and domain-specific errors consistently. codex-api/src/endpoint/realtime_websocket/protocol.rs defines the common websocket protocol and routes inbound events to version-specific parsers. codex-api/src/images.rs and codex-api/src/search.rs provide the concrete schemas for image generation/editing and the multi-command search API.
